---
title: "Working Code Document"
author: "Group 7"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

\# Data Wrangling

```{r data_wrangling}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(readr)
library(ggdag)
library(mice)
library(VIM)
library(naniar)


load(file='obesity.Rda')

other_outcomes <- c("diabetes", "MI", "angina", "stroke", "death") 

# Data wrangling: remove unused outcomes + rows with NAs
data <- obesity %>%
  select(!other_outcomes) %>%
  na.omit()

# Mutate to binary 
obesity <- data %>%
  mutate(medhyper = case_when(
    medhyper == "no" ~ 0,
    medhyper == "yes" ~ 1,),
    CVD = case_when(
      CVD == "No" ~ 0,
      CVD == "Yes" ~ 1,),
    medstatin = case_when(
    medstatin == "no" ~ 0,
    medstatin == "yes" ~ 1,),
    medglucose = case_when(
    medglucose == "no" ~ 0,
    medglucose == "yes" ~ 1,),
    current.smoking = case_when(
      current.smoking == "no" ~ 0,
      current.smoking == "yes" ~ 1,))

## Transform variables 
# Adiposity
plot(obesity$bmi, obesity$waist)
adiposity_model <- lm(bmi ~ waist, data = obesity)
summary(adiposity_model)

obesity$adiposity <- scale(obesity$bmi) + scale(obesity$waist)

# BP
plot(obesity$SYSBP, obesity$DIABP)
bp_model <- lm(SYSBP ~ DIABP, data = obesity)
summary(bp_model)

obesity$MAP <- obesity$DIABP + (obesity$SYSBP - obesity$DIABP)/3

# Alcohol 
obesity$alcohol <- case_when(obesity$alcohol > 5 ~ 6,
          obesity$alcohol == 5 ~ 5,
          obesity$alcohol == 4 ~ 4,
          obesity$alcohol == 3 ~ 3,
          obesity$alcohol == 2 ~ 2,
          obesity$alcohol == 1 ~ 1,
          obesity$alcohol == 0 ~ 0) 

# SES
obesity$SES <- ifelse(obesity$income == "low or normal" & obesity$education == "other", 1,
                      ifelse(obesity$education == "high" & obesity$income == "high", 3, 2))

# Quick look if populations are comparable for pre-treatment covariates
## Age
p1 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = age, fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Age") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

## Sex
p2 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = factor(sex), fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Sex") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())


## Adiposity 
p3 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = adiposity, fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Adiposity") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())


## BP
p4 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = MAP, fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "MAP") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

## Smoking
p5 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = factor(current.smoking), fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Smoking") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

## Alcohol
p6 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = factor(alcohol), fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Alcohol") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

## Statins
p7 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = factor(medstatin), fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Statins") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

## Glucose
p8 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = factor(medglucose), fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "Glucose Meds") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

p9 <- ggplot(obesity, aes(x = factor(SES), fill = factor(medhyper))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(x = "SES") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "MedHyper",
    values = c("0" = "#F8766D", "1" = "#00BFC4")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

# Combine plots
pop_plots <- list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7, p8, p9)

wrap_plots(pop_plots, nrow = 3) +
plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
theme(legend.position = "bottom")

```

## DAG

```{r dag}
library(ggdag)
library(dagitty)
library(ggplot2)


obesity_dag <- dagify(
  Adiposity ~ Age + Sex + SES,
  BP ~ Age + Sex + Adiposity + Smoking + Alcohol + SES,
  MedHyper ~ BP + SES,
  Smoking ~ Alcohol + SES,
  CVD ~ Age + Sex + Smoking + Alcohol + Adiposity + BP + MedHyper + SES,
  
  exposure = "MedHyper",
  outcome = "CVD",
  
  coords = list(
    x = c(
      Age = 2,
      Sex = 6,
      Smoking = 2,
      Alcohol = 6,
      Adiposity = 4,
      SES = 4,
      BP = 2,
      MedHyper = 4,
      CVD = 6
    ),
    
    y = c(
      Age = 3,
      Sex = 3,
      Smoking = 1,
      Alcohol = 1,
      Adiposity = 3,
      SES = 2,
      BP = 2,
      MedHyper = 1.25,
      CVD = 2
    )
  )
)

ggdag(obesity_dag, text_size = 2.4) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank())



adjustmentSets(obesity_dag)


ggdag_adjustment_set(
  obesity_dag,
  text = TRUE,
  text_size = 2.4
) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank()
    )
```

# Identifying confounders

## T-tests

```{r t-tests}
#t.test(obesity$age ~ obesity$medhyper) #significant
#t.test(obesity$adiposity ~ obesity$medhyper) #significant
t.test(obesity$MAP ~ obesity$medhyper) #significant
```

## Chi square

```{r chi square}
#chisq.test(obesity$sex, obesity$medhyper) #significant 
#chisq.test(obesity$current.smoking, obesity$medhyper) #not significant 
#chisq.test(obesity$alcohol, obesity$medhyper) #significant 
#chisq.test(obesity$medstatin, obesity$medhyper) #significant
#chisq.test(obesity$medglucose, obesity$medhyper) #significant 
chisq.test(obesity$SES, obesity$medhyper) #significant 
```

# Outcome regression

```{r outcome_regression}
glm = glm(CVD ~ medhyper + 
            #age + medhyper*age +
            #sex + medhyper*sex +
            #adiposity + medhyper*adiposity + 
            MAP + medhyper*MAP +
            #current.smoking + medhyper*current.smoking +
            #alcohol + current.smoking*alcohol +
            #medstatin +  medhyper*medstatin +
            #medglucose + medhyper*medglucose, 
            SES + medhyper*SES,
          data = obesity, family = binomial)
summary(glm)
```

# G-formula

## Step 1

```{r g-formula}
g_model = glm(CVD ~ medhyper + 
            #age + medhyper*age +
            #sex + medhyper*sex +
            #adiposity + medhyper*adiposity + 
            MAP + medhyper*MAP +
            #current.smoking + medhyper*current.smoking +
            #alcohol + current.smoking*alcohol +
            #medstatin +  medhyper*medstatin +
            #medglucose + medhyper*medglucose, 
            SES + medhyper*SES,
          data = obesity, family = binomial)
summary(g_model)
```

## Step 2

```{r step 2}
obesity1 = obesity
obesity1$medhyper = 1
YE1 = predict(g_model, newdata = obesity1, type="response")
mean_YE1 = mean(YE1, na.rm = T)
```

```{r step 2.5}
obesity0 = obesity
obesity0$medhyper = 0
YE0 = predict(g_model, newdata = obesity0, type="response")
mean_YE0 = mean(YE0, na.rm = T)

```

## Step 3

```{r step 3}
mean_YE1- mean_YE0 #average treatment effect (ATE)

(mean_YE1/(1-mean_YE1))/(mean_YE0/(1-mean_YE0)) #causal odds ratio
```

# Bootstrap to get the CI

```{r bootstrap}
set.seed(123)
B = 1000
ATE_boot = numeric(B)
OR_boot  = numeric(B)

n = nrow(obesity)

for (b in 1:B) {

  #Resample data
  index = sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE) 
  data_b = obesity[index, ] 

  #Refit model
g_model_b = glm(CVD ~ medhyper + 
            #age + medhyper*age +
            #sex + medhyper*sex +
            #adiposity + medhyper*adiposity + 
            MAP + medhyper*MAP +
            #current.smoking + medhyper*current.smoking +
            #alcohol + current.smoking*alcohol +
            #medstatin +  medhyper*medstatin +
            #medglucose + medhyper*medglucose, 
            SES + medhyper*SES,
          data = obesity, family = binomial)
  #Predict medhyper = 1
  data1 = data_b
  data1$medhyper = 1
  p1 = predict(g_model_b, newdata = data1, type="response")
  m1 = mean(p1, na.rm = T)

  #Predict medhyper = 0
  data0 = data_b
  data0$medhyper = 0
  p0 = predict(g_model_b, newdata = data0, type="response")
  m0 = mean(p0, na.rm = T)

  #Store estimands
  ATE_boot[b] = m1 - m0
  OR_boot[b]  = (m1/(1-m1)) / (m0/(1-m0))
}

mean(ATE_boot, na.rm = T)
mean(OR_boot, na.rm = T)

quantile(ATE_boot, c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = T)
quantile(OR_boot,  c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = T)
```

## Positivity

```{r positivity}
table(obesity$sex, obesity$medhyper)
table(obesity$current.smoking, obesity$medhyper)
table(obesity$alcohol, obesity$medhyper)
table(obesity$medstatin, obesity$medhyper)
table(obesity$medglucose, obesity$medhyper)
```

## Visualization

```{r visualisation}
# Plots (can find prettier colours at https://r-charts.com/colors/)
boots_df <- data.frame(
  ATE = ATE_boot,
  OR = OR_boot
)

ATE_plot <- ggplot(boots_df, aes(x = ATE)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey85") +
  labs(title = "Bootstrap ATE",
       x = "Risk Difference",
       y = NULL) +
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = mean(ATE_boot)),
    color = "slateblue4", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = quantile(ATE_boot, 0.025)),
    color = "slateblue1", linewidth = 0.5, linetype ="dashed") +
    geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = quantile(ATE_boot, 0.975)),
    color = "slateblue1", linewidth = 0.5, linetype ="dashed") +
  theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = 'black', linewidth = 0.5))

OR_plot <- ggplot(boots_df, aes(x = OR)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey85") +
  labs(title = "Bootstrap OR",
       x = "Odds Ratio",
       y = NULL) +
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = mean(OR_boot)),
    color = "tomato4", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = quantile(OR_boot, 0.025)),
    color = "tomato1", linewidth = 0.5, linetype ="dashed") +
    geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = quantile(OR_boot, 0.975)),
    color = "tomato1", linewidth = 0.5, linetype ="dashed") +
  theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = 'black', linewidth = 0.5))

wrap_plots(list(ATE_plot, OR_plot))
```

```{r summary results}
# Create a summary data frame
results_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Average Treatment Effect (ATE)", "Odds Ratio (OR)"),
  Estimate = c(mean(ATE_boot, na.rm = TRUE), mean(OR_boot, na.rm = TRUE)),
  Lower_CI = c(quantile(ATE_boot, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE), quantile(OR_boot, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)),
  Upper_CI = c(quantile(ATE_boot, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE), quantile(OR_boot, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE))
)

# Format for display
results_table$Formatted <- sprintf("%.3f (95%% CI: %.3f, %.3f)", 
                                   results_table$Estimate, 
                                   results_table$Lower_CI, 
                                   results_table$Upper_CI)

print(results_table[, c("Metric", "Formatted")])
```
## Missing data


```{r}
#------------------Data wrangling on incomplete data--------------------------------------------------------------------
load(file='obesity.Rda')
obesity_incomplete = obesity


# Mutate to binary 
obesity_incomplete = obesity_incomplete %>%
  mutate(medhyper = case_when(
    medhyper == "no" ~ 0,
    medhyper == "yes" ~ 1,),
    CVD = case_when(
      CVD == "No" ~ 0,
      CVD == "Yes" ~ 1,))

## Transform variables 

# Adiposity
plot(obesity_incomplete$bmi, obesity_incomplete$waist)
adiposity_model <- lm(bmi ~ waist, data = obesity_incomplete)
summary(adiposity_model)

obesity_incomplete$adiposity <- scale(obesity_incomplete$bmi) + scale(obesity_incomplete$waist)

# make Adiposity a numeric vector
obesity_incomplete$adiposity = as.numeric(scale(obesity_incomplete$bmi)) + as.numeric(scale(obesity_incomplete$waist))

# BP
plot(obesity_incomplete$SYSBP, obesity_incomplete$DIABP)
bp_model <- lm(SYSBP ~ DIABP, data = obesity_incomplete)
summary(bp_model)

obesity_incomplete$MAP <- obesity_incomplete$DIABP + (obesity_incomplete$SYSBP - obesity_incomplete$DIABP)/3
dev.off()

#-----------------NA pattern visualization ------------------------------------------------------------------

missing_pattern = md.pattern(obesity_incomplete)
missing_pattern

aggr(obesity_incomplete, numbers = TRUE, sortVars = TRUE)
# (diabetes - CVD - angina - death) are the variables where we find NAs together consistently



# -------------------testing--------------------------------
#test wheter the data is MCAR (apparently you can only do this when data is normally distributed...)

test = mcar_test(obesity_incomplete)
print(test) # is significant --> not MCAR

obesity_incomplete$miss_CVD = is.na(obesity_incomplete$CVD)


# see if missingness depends on other variables
model = glm(miss_CVD ~ medhyper + 
            age + sex + adiposity + MAP + current.smoking +
            alcohol + medstatin + medglucose,
            data = obesity_incomplete, family = binomial)
 
# missingness depends on other variables (age, sex) --> MAR?
 summary(model)
 
# check if missingness is related to age
ggplot(obesity_incomplete, aes(x = factor(miss_CVD), y = age)) +
  geom_boxplot() + labs(x = "Missingness of CVD", 
                        y = "Age", 
                        title = "Age vs Missingness in CVD") +
                        scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Non-missing", "Missing"))

odds_age = exp(coef(model)['age'])
odds_age # for 1 increase of year the odds to get cvd increases by 7.9%

# so its plausible to assume that the missingness is due to increase in age

# Just remove the rows with NAs since its 2% of our data missing

```


```{r weighting}
library(survey)
library(cobalt)
# Fit propensity model with all covariates
propensity_model <- glm(medhyper ~ age + sex + current.smoking + alcohol + medstatin + medglucose + adiposity + 
                          MAP + SES,
                        family = binomial, 
                        data = obesity)
summary(propensity_model)

# Calculate propensity
obesity$ps<-fitted.values(propensity_model)

# explore propensity
summary(obesity$ps)

# histogram
par(mfrow=c(2,1))
hist(obesity$ps[obesity$medhyper == 1], xlim=c(0,1),main="MedHyper", xlab="Propensity score")
hist(obesity$ps[obesity$medhyper == 0], xlim=c(0,1),main="no MedHyper", xlab="Propensity score")

# weights
obesity$ipw <- ifelse(obesity$medhyper == 1, 1/obesity$ps, 1/(1-obesity$ps)) 
summary(obesity$ipw)

# check for balance
variables <- c("age", "sex", "current.smoking", "alcohol", "medstatin", "medglucose", "adiposity", "MAP", "SES")
covariates <- obesity[, variables]
bal.tab(covariates, treat = obesity$medhyper, weights = obesity$ipw ,method = "weighting", un=TRUE)

#plot
love.plot(covariates, treat = obesity$medhyper, weights = obesity$ipw ,method = "weighting",
          binary = "std", threshold = .1)

# outcome table
table(obesity$CVD)


# weighted mean outcome in the treated
mean1.ipw <-weighted.mean(obesity$CVD[obesity$medhyper== 1], w=obesity$ipw[obesity$medhyper== 1])
mean1.ipw



# weighted mean outcome in the untreated
mean0.ipw <-weighted.mean(obesity$CVD[obesity$medhyper== 0], w=obesity$ipw[obesity$medhyper== 0])
mean0.ipw

# estimate of ATE
mean1.ipw-mean0.ipw


d.w <- svydesign(~1, weights = obesity$ipw, data = obesity)
fit.ipw <- svyglm(CVD ~ medhyper, design = d.w)
summary(fit.ipw)
# 95% CI
confint(fit.ipw)



# the 99 th percentile is
ipw2.p99 <-quantile(obesity$ipw,0.99)
ipw2.p99

obesity$ipw2.trunc <- ifelse(obesity$ipw>ipw2.p99, ipw2.p99, obesity$ipw)
summary(obesity$ipw2.trunc)


# and calculate the ATE with the truncated weights

d.w.trunc <- svydesign(~1, weights = obesity$ipw2.trunc, data = obesity)
fit.ipw.trunc <- svyglm(CVD ~ medhyper, design = d.w.trunc)
summary(fit.ipw.trunc)



# calculate numerator of stabilized weights (Pr(treatment ="RHC"))

numerator.model<-glm(medhyper ~ 1 , family = binomial,  data = obesity)

# Calculate PR(treatment =="RHC")
medhyper_yes <- fitted.values(numerator.model)
#stabilized weight
obesity$ipw2_stab <- ifelse(obesity$medhyper== 1, medhyper_yes/obesity$ps, (1-medhyper_yes)/(1-obesity$ps)) 
summary(obesity$ipw2_stab)
```

```{r matching}
library(MatchIt)
# 1:1 NN PS matching without replacement
match.out1 <- matchit(medhyper ~ age + sex + current.smoking + alcohol + medstatin + medglucose + adiposity + 
                          MAP + SES, data=obesity, method = "nearest", distance = "glm")

match.out1

summary(match.out1)

# w/ caliper and covariates
match.out2 <- matchit(medhyper ~ age + sex + current.smoking + alcohol + medstatin + medglucose + adiposity + 
                          MAP + SES, data=obesity, method = "nearest", distance = "glm", caliper = 0.1)

match.out2
summary(match.out2)

match.data <- match.data(match.out2)


table(match.data$medhyper, match.data$CVD) 
tab<- prop.table(table(match.data$medhyper, match.data$CVD) , margin=1)
tab


# w/ ps
match.out3 <- matchit(medhyper ~ ps, data=obesity, method = "nearest", distance = "glm")
summary(match.out3)

match.data <- match.data(match.out3)
table(match.data$medhyper, match.data$CVD) 
tab<- prop.table(table(match.data$medhyper, match.data$CVD) , margin=1)
tab
```

```{r messy stuff}
mean_bp <- mean(obesity$MAP)
propensity_model <- glm(medhyper ~ age + sex + current.smoking + alcohol + medstatin + medglucose + adiposity + SES,
                        family = binomial, 
                        data = obesity[obesity$MAP > mean_bp,])
summary(propensity_model)

propensity_model1 <- glm(medhyper ~ age + sex + current.smoking + alcohol + medstatin + medglucose + adiposity + SES,
                        family = binomial, 
                        data = obesity[obesity$MAP < mean_bp,])
summary(propensity_model1)


```
